title: #eng-infra: 6472105432
preview:
Micah Nguyen: heads-up — after the 03:30 rolling restart window I saw a tempstore IO latency spike in us-west1, GPU attaches started failing with attach timeouts and a bunch of pending pods. Stacktrace-ish logs attached below. Anyone seen this pattern?

Ava Chen: :eyes: can you paste the iostat / dstat snippet? also...
text:
Micah Nguyen: heads-up — after the 03:30 rolling restart window I saw a tempstore IO latency spike in us-west1, GPU attaches started failing with attach timeouts and a bunch of pending pods. Stacktrace-ish logs attached below. Anyone seen this pattern?

Ava Chen: :eyes: can you paste the iostat / dstat snippet? also which janitor/cleaner cron ran?

Micah Nguyen: iostat 1 5 showed crazy await on nvme0n1p1:
```
Device: tps kB_read/s kB_wrtn/s kB_read kB_wrtn
nvme0n1 50.0 1200.0 9800.0 6000 49000
```
And kube attach logs: `AttachVolume timed out after 120s waiting for device` — lots of tempfile cleanup activity in syslog. Cron job `janitor-sweep` started 03:32 on many nodes (we rolled 03:25-03:40).

Dmitri Petrov: rolling and janitor overlapped — we had the janitor schedule at */5 during the deploy patch, my bad. It forks up to 12 workers by default. Config snippet: `JOBS=12` in /etc/jan...

text:
Ethan Park: Heads-up: USW1 rack-11 showing repeated p95 spikes and a thermal uptick since 01:08. Looks like a single tenant doing aggressive multi-stream prefetch + long decode.

Rina Sato: telemetry matches. NIC bursts and HBM pinned. Which tenant?

Ethan Park: tid-4147 (partner eval). No dedicated carve. InfraBot alerted at 01:10.

InfraBot: ALERT rack-11: gpu_temp=83C, nic_tx=920MB/s, slab_evictions +340%. Ticket INC-10112 created.

Samir Khan: short-term mitigation: soft-fence into noisy-pool + apply egress policer and reduce concurrency. We have knobs: --egress_kbps and --max_streams.

Ava Lopez: please run conservative values first so we don't break the job: egress 650000 kbps, max_streams=2.

Samir Khan: running now:
```
allocatorctl set-tenant-policy --tenant=tid-4147 --pool=noisy-rack-pool --egress_kbps=650000 --max_streams=2
```
InfraBot: ACTION applied.

(45s) InfraBot: pol...

text:
Alice Nguyen: Heads-up — we just hit an onboarding blocker for a new customer in eastus2. Need NVv4 / A10G-style quota increase (1-2 nodes to start, target 8 within 2 weeks) + additional public IPs and accelerated networking for the VNET. Opened an Azure case but want to coordinate escalation. Case created: 210123456789.
Ben Carter: Case #? subscription? Also are these dedicated reserved nodes or spot?
Alice Nguyen: case 210123456789 (linked in portal). Subscription ID: xxxxx-aaaa-1111. We need reserved-pref capacity but can accept spot for the initial burst. Main constraint is latency for customers in eastus2, so prefer same region.
Priya Rao: Cost note — NVv4 reserved will be cheaper long-term but we should validate perf vs A100-ish SKUs first. If azure can't supply NVv4 at scale in eastus2, consider euapac fallback or fallback to A100 in nearby region.
Diego Morales: Adding: custom...

```
text:
Irene Choi: Heads up: we’ve got a driver/CUDA mismatch across the H100 clusters that’s biting the FP8/INT8 benchmark runs.

Irene Choi: Seeing some runs landing on `h100-gke-us-east1b` with NVIDIA driver 535.x, but the benchmark container is `cuda12.4`.

Grace Kim: that explains the failures I hit. got:
```
CUDA error: CUDA driver version is insufficient for CUDA runtime version
```
…only on some nodes.

Isabella Rossi: +1, and even when it doesn’t hard fail, I’m seeing massive variance + first-run spikes that look like JIT/compat behavior.

Vanessa Ortiz: Can you paste `nvidia-smi` from a “bad” node vs “good” node?

Rina Kobayashi: From the runner logs (same git SHA, same args):

Rina Kobayashi:
Bad node:
```
$ nvidia-smi
Driver Version: 535.146.02 CUDA Version: 12.2
GPU Name Persistence-M
0 H100 SXM5 On
```

Rina Kobayashi: Good node:
```
Driver Version: 550.54.14 CUDA Version: 12.4...

title: #eng-infra: 1770669135
preview:
Peter Holtz: seeing burn-rate alerts come in more frequently on Dedicated during burst (esp EU). can we align on what "capacity mitigation" means in practice? like, do we have an explicit headroom target per pool + when do we page Fleet Ops vs just traffic-shape?

Aisha Bello: +1. from SRE side we can do routing kno...
text:
Peter Holtz: seeing burn-rate alerts come in more frequently on Dedicated during burst (esp EU). can we align on what "capacity mitigation" means in practice? like, do we have an explicit headroom target per pool + when do we page Fleet Ops vs just traffic-shape?

Aisha Bello: +1. from SRE side we can do routing knobs, but we need a clear contract from fleet: "if Dedicated tier is burning, we protect it first" and what actions are allowed (borrow capacity? preempt hosted?)

Neha Kapoor: yep, we need to be explicit. Dedicated is SLA-bearing, Hosted is best-effort. If we’re in a budget burn state for Dedicated, infra posture should be: (1) preserve dedicated p95/p99, (2) shed/route hosted elsewhere before we violate dedicated.

Miles Thornton: can we frame this as a runbook section with triggers? e.g. burn rate > 2x for 30m OR p95 latency breach in a region => "capacity event".

Rafael ...

title: #eng-infra: 1768469814
preview:
Victor Alvarez: heads up, we’re proposing DNS TTL reductions for regional failover (Hosted + some Dedicated endpoints). Want a quick risk assessment here before we merge the tf changes.

Victor Alvarez: current state (most public records): TTL=60s (a few are 300s still). Proposal: drop to 20s for the failover-weight...
text:
Victor Alvarez: heads up, we’re proposing DNS TTL reductions for regional failover (Hosted + some Dedicated endpoints). Want a quick risk assessment here before we merge the tf changes.

Victor Alvarez: current state (most public records): TTL=60s (a few are 300s still). Proposal: drop to 20s for the failover-weighted records, keep everything else at 60s.

Irene Choi: 20s is… aggressive but maybe fine. Can you clarify which records exactly? `api.redwoodinference.com` is behind the geo/latency policy right?

Victor Alvarez: yep. Specifically:
- `api.redwoodinference.com` (latency/weighted policy)
- `stream.api.redwoodinference.com`
- `dedicated.<region>.redwoodinference.com` NOT in scope (those are customer-specific and already pinned)
- a couple internal healthcheck CNAMEs used by probes

Miles Thornton: +1 to being careful. Remember TTL is only one lever; resolvers don’t always respe...

title: #eng-infra: 1765123456
preview:
Ava Chen: quick ask — can we add a heatmap that highlights GPU slot sparsity across racks and regions? seeing weird cold pockets in eu-west.
Tom Briggs: yup, we should do a heatmap + a sparklines row. want it to surface both utilization and "sparsity" (slots idle > 20% for > 30m)
Ava Chen: exactly. aim is to correla...
text:
Ava Chen: quick ask — can we add a heatmap that highlights GPU slot sparsity across racks and regions? seeing weird cold pockets in eu-west.
Tom Briggs: yup, we should do a heatmap + a sparklines row. want it to surface both utilization and "sparsity" (slots idle > 20% for > 30m)
Ava Chen: exactly. aim is to correlate sparsity with thermal deltas and cost (warm-slotting wasted cycles)
Priya Nair: FYI we also need to treat eu-west with a different SLO — smaller fleet and tighter latency reqs. alert thresholds can't be same as us-west.
Jorge Ruiz: re: query — here's a starting promql I used locally:
```
sum by (rack, region) (rate(gpu_utilization_seconds_total[5m])) / sum by (rack, region) (machine_cpu_cores)
```
but better to use node-level allocatable vram and nonzero allocations: 
```
(sum by(rack,region)(gpu_allocated_vram_bytes) / sum by(rack,region)(gpu_total_vram_bytes))
```
Tom ...

title: #eng-infra: 1762643912
preview:
Irene Choi: heads up, for hot-route protection we need to settle which fleet signals are actually reliable for admission control triggers. seeing debate in reviews: GPU saturation vs queue depth vs scheduler lag. want to lock a recommendation this week + note region-specific thresholds.

Vanessa Ortiz: +1. GPU % is ...
text:
Irene Choi: heads up, for hot-route protection we need to settle which fleet signals are actually reliable for admission control triggers. seeing debate in reviews: GPU saturation vs queue depth vs scheduler lag. want to lock a recommendation this week + note region-specific thresholds.

Vanessa Ortiz: +1. GPU % is the one everyone reaches for but it lies a lot in practice (esp in us-east). queue depth seems closer to “customer pain” but also depends on batching settings.

Rafael Mendes: from SRE view: the main thing is signals that correlate with p95/p99 latency BEFORE we’re on fire. GPU util tends to peg at 95% during healthy steady state for some pools.

Keiko Yamamoto: also autoscaler can mask things. gpu-util can be high while we’re scaling out, so if we shed based on util we’d shed too aggressively.

Jamal Pierce: i pulled a quick snapshot from last week burst (us-east) — GPU ut...

text:
Sahana Iyer: heads up: the signature verification integration tests are now the slowest step in `private-installer` CI 😬

Sahana Iyer: `bundle-verify-integration` job went from ~6m to ~28-35m after we added tamper/replay/downgrade cases. We need to get this back under ~10m or we’re gonna start timing out in release train.

ci-bot: Buildkite build #118244 for redwood-private-installer / main failed
ci-bot: Step `bundle-verify-integration` exceeded soft timeout (30m). Log: https://buildkite.example.redwood/...

Devon Price: looking at the logs now. a lot of time is `sha256sum` over big fixtures + re-generating offline bundle each test case.

Ethan Park: yeah, each test currently:
1) builds bundle (tar + generate manifest)
2) signs manifest
3) unpacks
4) modifies artifact
5) runs preflight

Ethan Park: so we’re doing the expensive steps N times.

Jordan Lee: manifest canonicalization st...

text:
Maria Gomez: FYI — noticed a ~28% bump in GPU spend for Feb compared to forecast. Most of it shows up as "untagged" in the billing export. Anyone else seeing this?
Kevin Li: can you paste the billing export link? we should run the usual BigQuery grouping.
Maria Gomez: https://console.cloud.google.com/billing/exports — I exported to bigquery this morning. top-line is GPUs in us-central1 and europe-west4 appearing under project:infra-shared.
Sachin Rao: infra-shared is our shared GPU pool for sandbox teams, right? sounds like workloads are spinning up without cost center tags.
Lina Park: yep, some onboarding scripts default to project-level creds and don't set labels. we need enforcement + backfill.
Ben Carter: quick reproduction: my local job uses the shared pool and i didn't get prompted to set label. we should default a label on the compute template.
InfraBot: Deploy 17592 succeeded ...

preview:

Jonas Weber: Yep. Publishing regions: us-east, eu-west, apac-sg. Models: Llama 3.1 8B/70B, Qwen2.5 7B/72B, Mixtral 8x7B. We want to start sw...
text:
Vanessa Ortiz: Need to lock capacity reservations for benchmark sweeps in eu-west + APAC. @Jonas Weber @Rina Kobayashi can you confirm model list + rough run window next week?

Jonas Weber: Yep. Publishing regions: us-east, eu-west, apac-sg. Models: Llama 3.1 8B/70B, Qwen2.5 7B/72B, Mixtral 8x7B. We want to start sweeps Mon-Wed and rerun if stability gate trips.

Rina Kobayashi: Runner can do region sweep matrix but I need to know what hardware we’re standardizing on per model. If we mix instance types across regions, p95 variance gets messy.

Vanessa Ortiz: That’s the main issue: eu-west has decent H100 coverage; apac-sg is constrained on H100 and the A100 pool is fragmented.

Harish Reddy: Adding current fleet reality:
- eu-west: h100x8 nodes available (80GB), plus a100x8 (80GB) as fallback
- apac-sg: limited h100x8, more a100x8 but several are already allocated to customer Dedicate...

title: #eng-infra: 1748572103
preview:
Vanessa Ortiz: kicking off a quick thread to de-risk bounds implementation before we merge the autoscaler changes. Need alignment on (1) edge cases + semantics (2) defaulting strategy for existing Dedicated deployments (3) who owns migration/backfill. I’m looking at ENG-22140 + ENG-22153.

Keiko Yamamoto: +1. I’m in...
text:
Vanessa Ortiz: kicking off a quick thread to de-risk bounds implementation before we merge the autoscaler changes. Need alignment on (1) edge cases + semantics (2) defaulting strategy for existing Dedicated deployments (3) who owns migration/backfill. I’m looking at ENG-22140 + ENG-22153.

Keiko Yamamoto: +1. I’m in the autoscaler loop code now. Biggest question: do we expect control plane to always send explicit `min_replicas` / `max_replicas`, or can they be null? i can clamp either way but need deterministic behavior.

Benji Okafor: schema PR (ENG-22141) is leaning nullable fields: `bounds_enabled` + `min_replicas?` + `max_replicas?` + cooldowns. Validation: if bounds_enabled=true then require both min/max and enforce min<=max. If bounds_enabled=false, we treat min/max as ignored even if present.

Jordan Lee: from policy engine POV, we can compute an “effective policy” snapshot tha...

text:
Bruno Silva: Heads up: Terraform module changes for KMS are ready for review. This is the follow-on to the least-privilege work + rotation role split. Draft PR: https://github.com/redwood-terraform/pull/611 ("add least-privilege KMS IAM modules").

Bruno Silva: TL;DR
- New submodules: `kms_runtime_role` (Decrypt/DataKey) and `kms_rotation_role` (DescribeKey + CreateGrant/PutKeyPolicy bits depending on cloud).
- Default behavior stays close to existing, but we *will* add new outputs and a couple of variables.
- Need to decide version bump + compatibility notes for existing Private customers.

Jordan Lee: looking now. Do we currently have customers pinning the module version in their Private deploy repos? If yes, we need to be careful about a breaking major.

Bruno Silva: yep, most Private customers pin `redwood/private` terraform modules. Some are pinned by installer-generated `version...

title: #eng-infra: 1747075433
preview:
Connor O'Brien: quick q for min-pack: do we *need* OTLP log export in v1, or is “supported sinks = Splunk HEC + Elastic/OpenSearch + (maybe) Loki” enough? trying to lock scope for the release branch.

Logan Wright: depends what we mean by “OTLP”. If we ship an otel-collector in the cluster and say “send to your OTLP...
text:
Connor O'Brien: quick q for min-pack: do we *need* OTLP log export in v1, or is “supported sinks = Splunk HEC + Elastic/OpenSearch + (maybe) Loki” enough? trying to lock scope for the release branch.

Logan Wright: depends what we mean by “OTLP”. If we ship an otel-collector in the cluster and say “send to your OTLP endpoint”, that’s a whole support surface (auth, retries, tls, backpressure, perf). For v1 I’d rather standardize on 1 collector + 2-3 explicit outputs.

Colin O'Donnell: +1. from customer calls: 80% ask “Splunk HEC?” / “Elastic?” and then “can you not break our cluster”. OTLP comes up mostly as “we already have an otel pipeline” but they usually can adapt if we give them JSON logs and a stable namespace.

Allison Grant: reminder: standards doc currently says “provide export options suitable for private envs (SIEM/Splunk/ELK/Loki/OTLP endpoints)” but it doesn’t say *all* a...

title: #eng-infra: 1746384109
preview:
Sean Gallagher: We need to land a default Prometheus TSDB sizing + retention rec for Private min pack. What are we comfortable shipping as "sane defaults" for typical footprints? Mostly concerned about PVC size + churn when folks have 15s scrapes.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Support keeps getting "Prometheus disk full" esca...
text:
Sean Gallagher: We need to land a default Prometheus TSDB sizing + retention rec for Private min pack. What are we comfortable shipping as "sane defaults" for typical footprints? Mostly concerned about PVC size + churn when folks have 15s scrapes.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Support keeps getting "Prometheus disk full" escalations, and we don’t have consistent guidance. Can we target 7d retention as default and document 15d/30d options?

Allison Grant: From standards side: minimum pack should be conservative on cardinality, but customers will still have node exporter + kube metrics + our runtime metrics. Default should prioritize stability over long retention. 7d time-based retention is fine, but we should also recommend `retention.size` as a guardrail.

Logan Wright: Agree. Also reminder: some Private installs use smaller clusters / single AZ w/ standard storage class. We should avoid recomm...

title: #eng-infra: 1741254992
preview: Sahana Iyer: We need to nail down CI secrets + signing keys for the runtime LTS release branches. Right now the release workflows can build fine, but publishing + signing is blocked because the secrets are only scoped to main and we don’t want to just “enable secrets on release/*” and call it a day. Any suggestions ...
text:
Sahana Iyer: We need to nail down CI secrets + signing keys for the runtime LTS release branches. Right now the release workflows can build fine, but publishing + signing is blocked because the secrets are only scoped to main and we don’t want to just “enable secrets on release/*” and call it a day. Any suggestions on reducing blast radius but still letting us publish LTS artifacts? (context: INT-3302)

Devon Price: +1. For LTS patch week we’ll need:
- push runtime images to registry (packages:write)
- generate checksums + provenance
- sign images + checksums (cosign + whatever we decide for tarballs)
We can’t put long-lived keys into repo secrets accessible from PR contexts.

Connor O'Brien: Release Eng POV: I’m fine with an extra manual approval step if it keeps us out of trouble. The patch cadence is monthly; we can live with “protected environment + required reviewers” as long as ...

title: #eng-infra: 1740648811
preview:
Harish Reddy: hey folks 👋 quick review request. runbook draft for provision-to-ready failures is in Confluence. before we mark it “oncall-ready” i want eyes on remediation ordering. right now it says “quarantine -> reimage -> drain” which feels wrong / risky.

Harish Reddy: link: https://confluence.redwoodinference...
text:
Harish Reddy: hey folks 👋 quick review request. runbook draft for provision-to-ready failures is in Confluence. before we mark it “oncall-ready” i want eyes on remediation ordering. right now it says “quarantine -> reimage -> drain” which feels wrong / risky.

Harish Reddy: link: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGINFRA/Provision-to-ready+failures+triage+runbook (WIP)

Vanessa Ortiz: yeah +1, that order can cause ugly surprises. if you reimage before drain/cordon, kube still thinks it has a node + we can get stuck terminating pods / misreported capacity. also if node is in serving pool, you can take a partial outage.

Rafael Mendes: +1 from SRE side. we should make the “safe default” path explicit. oncall should never start with reimage unless we’ve already removed it from traffic and captured artifacts.

Gabe Wilson: agreed. the orchestrator will also keep retrying u...

title: #eng-infra: 1740259101

text:
Logan Wright: Heads up / quick retro from the Sev1 earlier — telemetry query latency spiked hard right when incident-bot started pulling saturation cards.

Grafana Bot: Alert triggered: telemetry-query-service p95_latency_ms > 5000 for 10m (current: 8120ms). Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/telemetry-query-service

Rafael Mendes: +1, oncall perspective: bot was timing out and then responders started manually refreshing dashboards -> made it worse. Need guardrails.

Miles Thornton: I pulled logs from incident-bot. We went from ~3 qps to ~70 qps in ~2 mins when people hit “Refresh signals” in multiple threads. Also Slack interactive actions retried a few times when ack was late.

Devon Price: ack deadline for Slack interactive is 3s; we were doing live query before ack in a couple paths. That causes retry storms. We can flip to ack-immediately + async update, but still need...

text:
Irene Choi: Trying to close out the provisioning checklist deltas for Premium tier today. Need crisp differences vs Plus/Standard: warm pool, spare nodes, HA. Can folks sanity check?

Irene Choi: Draft deltas (Premium only):
- Warm pool (pre-warmed) capacity maintained in-region
- N+1 spare node(s) in the Dedicated pool
- HA config (multi-AZ) + failover runbook validated
- Additional perf validation (burn-in)

Vanessa Ortiz: +1 on having it explicit. For warm pool: can we define as “1 node equivalent per region per Premium customer” or is it per shape class?

Jamal Pierce: per customer warm pool is gonna be rough in us-east if they pick H100. Supply is tight. I’d rather define as “warm pool availability subject to shape availability” but that gets contract-y.

Harish Reddy: Operationally we can do warm pool as “one spare *slot*” that can be any compatible node, not necessarily identic...

text:
Sahana Iyer: seeing a bunch of CI flakes on the `private-upgrade-e2e` suite again. last 10 nightly runs: 4 failures, and at least 3 look non-deterministic (rerun passed). this is the ENG-4877 track but it’s now blocking a couple PRs due to required checks.

Sahana Iyer: failure modes from the last two:
- k8s cluster never reaches ready (kind) within 8m, then everything cascades
- snapshot hook step hangs (CSI) and times out
- intermittent `helm upgrade` failing w/ `context deadline exceeded` when waiting on pre-upgrade job

Sahana Iyer: question: do we add retries (rerun jobs / rerun steps) or should we invest in more deterministic fixtures first? trying to pick the right “stop the bleeding” move.

text:
Gabe Wilson: seeing NCCL post-provision smoke test flaking again in us-east A100. validation agent marks node as failed -> orchestrator retries -> sometimes passes on 2nd attempt. anyone else looking at this?

Gabe Wilson: log excerpt from one of the failures (from /var/log/redwood/validation/nccl-smoke.log):
```bash
+ timeout 90s bash -lc 'NCCL_DEBUG=INFO NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME=eth0 ./all_reduce_perf -b 8M -e 256M -f 2 -g 8'
NCCL version 2.18.3+cuda12.1
...
WARN NET/Socket : Connection closed by remote peer
INFO transport/net_socket.cc:67 -> 2
WARN init.cc:868 -> 2
ncclInternalError: Internal check failed.
Last error:
Error: failed to connect to 10.42.18.93<32415>
```

Vanessa Ortiz: 90s seems tight if the node is still warming up / ib devices settling. what’s the pass latency when it succeeds?

Gabe Wilson: on success, the test itself is like ~18-30s. but we have a few that take 70-85s ...

title: #eng-infra: 1739390021
preview:
Irene Choi: pulling folks into this thread re backup/snapshot mechanism for Private upgrades. need something that works across AWS EBS, generic CSI (EKS + on-prem), and fallback to logical backups. what’s the realistic lowest common denom?

Vanessa Ortiz: for VPC installs on AWS we can do EBS snapshots pretty reliab...
text:
Irene Choi: pulling folks into this thread re backup/snapshot mechanism for Private upgrades. need something that works across AWS EBS, generic CSI (EKS + on-prem), and fallback to logical backups. what’s the realistic lowest common denom?

Vanessa Ortiz: for VPC installs on AWS we can do EBS snapshots pretty reliably, but if PV is not EBS (gp3) then we’re back in CSI land. also: data plane caches are “nice to have” restore, control plane DB is “must be consistent”.

Fatima Noor: on-prem: no EBS, obviously. and not every customer cluster has CSI snapshot CRDs installed/enabled. some are on Ceph/Rook (supports snapshots), some on vSphere (depends), some on bare metal NFS (no snapshots). anything that *requires* VolumeSnapshot will be a blocker.

Bruno Silva: +1. I’ve seen air-gapped customers with storageclass = nfs-client provisioner. there is no snapshot capability. also no object st...

title: #eng-infra: 1739298841
preview:
Rafael Mendes: Heads up from yesterday’s incident review: incident-bot diagnostics caused a mini thundering herd on telemetry when multiple responders hit “refresh signals” in #incidents. We need hard bounds (rate limits, circuit breakers, backoff) before v2 goes wider.

Sean Gallagher: +1. This is exactly how we ge...
text:
Rafael Mendes: Heads up from yesterday’s incident review: incident-bot diagnostics caused a mini thundering herd on telemetry when multiple responders hit “refresh signals” in #incidents. We need hard bounds (rate limits, circuit breakers, backoff) before v2 goes wider.

Sean Gallagher: +1. This is exactly how we get cascading failures during Sev1/Sev0. What’s our current shape? Any global caps on diag queries?

Miles Thornton: Right now bot fans out to 3 backends (deploy-metadata, config-diff, telemetry-query). Telemetry is the big one: ~8–15 queries per card render depending on tenant/region selection. We have some basic per-process concurrency limit but no distributed/global limit.

Logan Wright: Telemetry side: the query gateway already has per-user limits, but bot uses a service identity so it bypasses “human” shaping. When the bot bursts it shows up as a single principal doing 2...

title: #eng-infra: 1739237721
preview:
Vanessa Ortiz: We need to lock quarantine policy this week so orchestrator + readiness svc can ship. Question: do we auto-quarantine after N validation failures, or always reimage + retry until it passes?

Harish Reddy: speaking from capacity ops: always reimage is expensive + slow, but auto-quarantine too aggressiv...
text:
Vanessa Ortiz: We need to lock quarantine policy this week so orchestrator + readiness svc can ship. Question: do we auto-quarantine after N validation failures, or always reimage + retry until it passes?

Harish Reddy: speaking from capacity ops: always reimage is expensive + slow, but auto-quarantine too aggressive can starve pools during expansions. We need a middle ground + operator override.

Jamal Pierce: +1, in big Dedicated adds we can’t afford 10–20% to quarantine on flaky checks. If quarantine rate spikes, we drop available throughput and support pings us.

Keiko Yamamoto: What’s the failure distribution from baseline? Are we talking deterministic failures (driver mismatch) vs transient (network jitter / registry timeouts)?

Vanessa Ortiz: Baseline notes: most common are kubelet join flake, NCCL smoke timeout, occasional driver parity mismatch when image channel drifts. Driv...

title: #eng-infra: 1738871991
preview:
Vanessa Ortiz: dropping this here for review before we lock defaults for GA. Need alignment on (1) fleet safety caps for burst pools and (2) global circuit breaker behavior. goal is: bursting never drags non-burst dedicated SLOs.

Vanessa Ortiz: current proposal (v3):
- Per-pool soft guardrail at 0.80 saturation (st...
text:
Vanessa Ortiz: dropping this here for review before we lock defaults for GA. Need alignment on (1) fleet safety caps for burst pools and (2) global circuit breaker behavior. goal is: bursting never drags non-burst dedicated SLOs.

Vanessa Ortiz: current proposal (v3):
- Per-pool soft guardrail at 0.80 saturation (start increasing deny % for *new* burst requests)
- Per-pool hard CB at 0.92 saturation for >=2m OR queue_depth >= 300 for >=60s
- Global fleet CB if ANY region/hw pool trips hard CB AND neighbor impact indicators show regression (more below)

Vanessa Ortiz: numbers we need to agree on:
- saturation thresholds: 0.80 / 0.92
- queue depth: 300
- CB open duration / cooldown: 10m open, then half-open with 10% admit for 5m

Keiko Yamamoto: +1 on having both saturation and queue depth. saturation alone lags when telemetry is stale.

Keiko Yamamoto: Q: what are we defining as satura...

text:
Vanessa Ortiz: seeing something weird in beta pool partitioning. canary stage went live (stage=1%) and queue depth spiked hard + 429s. looks like canary partition is starving capacity.

Vanessa Ortiz: pool: `dedicated-pool/lat-emb-prod` (beta), region `us-east-1`, reservation 8xH100. stages: 1%/10%/50%/100%. canary got 1 replica.

Irene Choi: do we have a graph? is this just bursty traffic or sustained? also did they have streaming on?

Vanessa Ortiz: grafana link (internal): https://grafana.redwood.internal/d/dedicated-rollout-stage?var-pool=lat-emb-prod&from=now-1h

Vanessa Ortiz: tl;dr at 22:11 UTC, stage promoted to canary. within ~2 min:
- canary `queue_depth_p95` jumps from ~0-3 -> 220+
- canary `request_drop_rate` to 2-5%
- overall pool 429s from ~0.1% -> 1.6% (mostly canary routed)

Peter Holtz: that’s consistent w/ tiny partition + sticky routing. if the canary partition has ...

title: #eng-infra: 1738791123
preview:
Vanessa Ortiz: trying to lock baseline metrics for “time-to-ready” before we build the new state machine. need alignment on: what exact timestamp = “allocated” and what exact timestamp = “ready”. also: what’s the source of truth so we don’t have 3 different numbers floating around.

Miles Thornton: +1. for SLI we ne...
text:
Vanessa Ortiz: trying to lock baseline metrics for “time-to-ready” before we build the new state machine. need alignment on: what exact timestamp = “allocated” and what exact timestamp = “ready”. also: what’s the source of truth so we don’t have 3 different numbers floating around.

Miles Thornton: +1. for SLI we need start/end events that are (a) consistent across regions/GPU types, (b) collectible today (even if messy), and (c) same semantics we’ll use post-sprint.

Aisha Bello: if we can’t get the canonical events yet, I’d rather define the SLI now and do “best-effort backfill” than pick something like terraform apply time.

Keiko Yamamoto: please not terraform apply time 🙃 that’s gonna include human delays + CI queueing + retries. also apply can be rerun.

Jamal Pierce: from capacity ops POV, “allocated” is basically when we see instance IDs created + tagged to the pool. but depe...

title: #eng-infra: 1738709033
preview:
Irene Choi: want to lock down region constraints for Dedicated staged rollouts. esp multi-region pools: what does “stage=10%” mean when pool is us-east + eu-west? and what if eu-west is impaired mid-stage?

Mina Haddad: +1. routing team needs a crisp rule. today Dedicated pools can be multi-region active/active with...
text:
Irene Choi: want to lock down region constraints for Dedicated staged rollouts. esp multi-region pools: what does “stage=10%” mean when pool is us-east + eu-west? and what if eu-west is impaired mid-stage?

Mina Haddad: +1. routing team needs a crisp rule. today Dedicated pools can be multi-region active/active with per-region weights. if we introduce stage partitions, we need to decide if stage split is global (10% of total traffic) vs per-region (10% in each region).

Vanessa Ortiz: infra angle: capacity is reserved per region. if you do “global 10%” you can accidentally put all canary replicas in one region (because that’s where alloc succeeds). then if that region is the hot path you’re effectively >10% user impact.

Ravi Menon: also if a region is partially impaired (GPU health issues, allocator can’t place, or AZ down), global math gets weird. the router can only send to where r...

text:
Irene Choi: seeing some weird variance in the shape benchmarks folks are using for the tier mapping convo. A100 80G vs H100 SXM vs L40S is not trending the way ppl expect. can we sanity check before we lock the catalog numbers?

Vanessa Ortiz: +1. sales is already asking “why is H100 only ~1.4x in this table” and I don’t want them quoting that. what runs are we looking at? link?

Nina Petrova: assuming you mean the latest bench sheet from the fleet drive (the one Sergio pulled into the draft catalog). the short-context runs are mostly compute bound, long-context runs flip to memory bw + kv cache behavior.

Harish Reddy: also heads up: a bunch of the A100s in us-east are 80GB PCIe, not SXM. the H100s are mixed too (some PCIe in eu-west). if the sheet isn’t separating pcie vs sxm, it’ll look “flat”.

Jamal Pierce: yeah, the capacity forecast assumes we can treat “H100 region” as one poo...

title: #eng-infra: 1738441123
preview:
Vanessa Ortiz: Kicking off sizing + initial rollout seq for Dedicated burst pools. Need alignment w/ Product on assumptions we can safely commit to (and what we should *not* promise). I’m thinking we start w/ 1 region + 1 hardware class (us-east H100) for beta. Thoughts?

Lauren Bishop: +1. For PRD we need an “initi...
text:
Vanessa Ortiz: Kicking off sizing + initial rollout seq for Dedicated burst pools. Need alignment w/ Product on assumptions we can safely commit to (and what we should *not* promise). I’m thinking we start w/ 1 region + 1 hardware class (us-east H100) for beta. Thoughts?

Lauren Bishop: +1. For PRD we need an “initial coverage” statement for beta/GA. Sales is already asking “which regions first” (ofc). What’s feasible given procurement + safety margins?

Jamal Pierce: From capacity planning: we can stand up a small managed burst pool in us-east H100 without risking baseline Dedicated supply. us-west H100 is tighter until late Q2. eu-west L40S is easier sooner but demand is less predictable.

Harish Reddy: Operationally we can only run game day + oncall playbooks in a limited set first. Preference: us-east H100 → us-west H100 → eu-west L40S. (Keeping hardware classes minimal early redu...
